lastUpdateSummary.headline: Visible Hormuz conditions still point to a near-standstill with selective Iranian-linked flow as the key exception.
lastUpdateSummary.body: Across the tracked sources reviewed this round, no new item added stronger operational evidence than the prior confirmed Iranian-linked liftings and selective-passage pattern already in history. The most durable dashboard read remains that mainstream commercial traffic is still heavily suppressed while residual movement is concentrated in politically approved, sanctioned, dark or partially visible channels.

last24hSummary.headline: The last 24 hours reinforce the split between near-zero visible transit and lower-visibility or selectively cleared movement.
last24hSummary.body: TankerTrackers, MarineTraffic, Kpler, Giovanni Staunovo, recent research coverage and the Hormuz Strait Monitor baseline all continue to support the same operational picture: visible tanker and gas-carrier transit remains close to zero, coalition escort capacity is still uncertain, and the signal that matters most for the dashboard is the gap between suppressed compliant traffic and residual Iranian-linked or politically filtered movement. No additional source reviewed this round cleared the threshold for a genuinely new promoted item by canonical URL and analytical value.
